entry.title: Re: What do the values 'AG, RG, RE, ZM' in OPD mean?
entry.content:
Please see below the explanation to the configuration step 'Set Up Partner Determination for Sales Document Header' (ID 101288):

You can convert the keys, that identify the partner functions in the original German system, to language-specific keys. The converted keys then appear according to the language in which the user logs on to to system.

If you work with multiple languages in your system, you must define language-dependent keys for the partner functions. For example, for the ship-to party, whose SAP key (internal key, in German) is "WE", you can define "SH" as the English key. As a result, the system shows "SH" for the ship-to party partner function when the system logon language is English.
For more information please see the SAP Help documentation Partner Control in Master Records and Documents.

Please refer to the Partner Function Conversion dialog structure for the respective partner function keys in your system.
The 'Partn. Funct.' column shows the SAP internal two-digits partner keys.
The 'Partn.Funct. (Language-specific)' column lists the language specific partner keys defined for the specific logon language in the column 'Language'.

  • English

  • German:

The 'Output Parameter Determination' app uses the two-digits SAP internal partner function keys.

You can access stories via proxy because story reporting doesn't respect proxy. You are effectively logging in as yourself even in proxy

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now library link opens in new window
entry.content:
Hi Joke,

I'm not certain but the first behaviour that you mention, i.e. the content opening in a second window on start up of a library, however, I suspect that the first window is authenticating the user and the second contains the content.  The second behaviour that you mention is correct.  If you link from a landing page to a Simulation and choose not to open the Simulation in a new window, it opens the Simulation in a virtual tab.  Refer to the image below.

 The number 2 in the top, right corner of the image indicates that the content is open in a second virtual tab.  If the user closes the virtual tab using the X, the original library is redisplayed.

Hope that helps.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: how to add multiple report in one report during run time
entry.content:
Difficult to provide a good response without a clear sense of the data model.
 If a single table you can simply get the data as multiple records. Otherwise, here are a few options:

Use a view/stored procedure/command to UNION the results into a single record set.
Use Subreports, suppressing blank ones.
Use multiple detail sections (Detail a, Detail b, ...) and sql expressions, with conditional suppress expressions.

entry.title: Re: Call API returns code 500
entry.content:
Sounds like you are missing a SOAPAction header, so the receiving system is unable to identify which action to take.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to prolong the starter system in S/4HA...
entry.content:
Hello,

Your Starter System will be taken away three months after the production system provisioning. However, if you want to keep the Starter System beyond that, it is around $500 per month. Talk to your Account Executive to get a firm price.

Regards,

George

entry.title: Handling Units not assigned in inspection lot
entry.content:
Hi,

A company with multiple branches across different countries uses SAP. One branch of the company conducted a shipment to another branch in a different country by sending goods in specific quantities, with each pallet containing a handling unit, using an outbound delivery to transfer the goods to the receiving branch.

Upon receiving these goods, the receiving branch processed a goods receipt for the inbound delivery. However, they deleted the handling units sent by the other branch, which then went to storage location 9000. The receiving branch (Branch B) then created new handling units, but these new units do not appear in the sampling report and also do not show up in the inspection lot.

I have placed the jar file into:

  • /Users/david/Library/Java/Extensions
  • /Library/Java/Extensions

I also followed https://me.sap.com/notes/0003415747, but still no luck I continue to get this error.  

What am I doing incorrect here and why has this become an issue now.

En nuestra empresa hemos acordado aplicar descuentos por pronto pago a proveedores para compras recurrentes. Al intentar configurar estas condiciones de pago y parametrizar los descuentos, nos dimos cuenta de que la opción no está disponible en el sistema.

Adjunto una imagen de referencia para que puedan tener una mejor idea de lo que estamos viendo.

entry.title: Re: FIORI Elements Id and Text annotation does not work i...
entry.content:
The reason is that the text field (Parnr) isn't being requested in the GET call of your table. You need to include is in the RequestAtLeast columns (which I haven't figured out how yet). 

To try this theory, add the text field field Parnr to the table, you'll see that your original column Parvw will correctly display the text as well.

entry.title: The Procurement Monthly - November 2024
entry.content:
A really full month, this month. The continuing importance of supplier relationships permeates throughout the reports this month, as organisations are continuing to find ways to manage the constant uncertainty. Longer term the reports talk to shortening and changing supply chains, moving toward more regionalisation, and therefore the need to drive visibility and better relationships becomes paramount.

AI also is mentioned frequently both in terms of how it will change the way procurement delivers its services, but also the way its organised and the future skill sets needed.

With recent geopolitical changes the impacts on procurement could be far reaching, how will sustainability continue to be prioritised, what do the changes mean for deglobalisation and how will contracts need to evolve to concentrate on what really matters?

AQPC Risk Management

 

AQPC published a couple of reports linked together this month around risk management. The first report stated that the biggest challenges that procurement professionals are facing right now. For example, respondents to their recent procurement survey said that their three biggest challenges are:

  • Shortages/delays in raw materials (47%)
  • Cybersecurity risks (40%)
  • Complexity in data management (34%).

The report goes into discussing controlling only that you can control and suggests that this includes measures to monitor suppliers that will help with these.

These measures include supplier responsiveness (915), on time delivery (90%) and quality (87%). Interestingly AQPC found a small but meaningful difference between direct and indirect procurement when it comes to these measures. Respondents who carry out direct procurement tend to focus on supplier quality the most (39% of respondents, followed by 37% for on-time delivery). For indirect procurement, the focus is more toward on-time delivery (36%), with quality a close second (34%). Both have price very low at 2/1%.

In additional report this month, AQPC looked specifically at risk in the supply chain, gathering insights from 316 respondents on selected risk management practices with over 80% stating that they have a system that provides a risk profile of suppliers.

A key part of risk management is scenario planning and then the relevant contingency planning that ensues and as part of this research it identified that most organisations do have a process to collect and review their suppliers contingency plans but only 36% are performing mitigations and improving them.  

Ivalua – Future of work

Ivalua conducted a study with 200 US based leaders around the future of work in procurement. It identified that 60% saw AI reshaping procurement by creating new roles and redefining existing ones with 48% seeing this as an opportunity and 11% as a threat.

Other key takeaways were:

  • 70% investing in upskilling programs and 68% in reskilling.
  • 71% reported their organisations are well prepared to face changes in the future of work

However obstacles included:

  • Different departmental priorities (57%)
  • Siloed information (44%)
  • Lack of clear communication channels (22%)

DPW – 10X Procurement

At DPW Amsterdam, DPW released details of a survey of over 200 global procurement leaders in associated with the University of Arkansas.

It identified that

  • There was a  30-35% shortfall in critical capabilities (Change management, digital acumen etc)
  • 20% of respondents are adopting or scaling AI within their procurement functions, and procurement processes remain only 50% automated on average

Procurement Leaders – The Big Picture

The 2024 Procurement Excellence Benchmarking Survey, , reveals that increased collaboration with business stakeholders, suppliers and legal teams – alongside a renewed emphasis on technology, operational efficiencies and organisational design – is enhancing procurement functions’ strategic value within their businesses

3 main themes emerged

Operational excellence: Procurement’s ability to operate an efficient and cost-effective organisation.

15% of organisations had separated strategic and tactical (execution) tasks by having dedicated focus and 85% had these tasks managed through a combined role

 

Value enablement: Procurement-generated organisational value by leveraging tools and technology.

30% are using new technologies while 34% believe there are missing pieces to add in – a further 25% are improving their utilisation of existing technology.

 

Growth strategies: Forward-looking trends and anticipated changes in the procurement landscape

57% have a medium or high engagement with their sales and marketing teams to lessen the silos of decision making.

Gartner

Gartner has been on fire this month! They have released several papers all citing information gathered by their CPO study.

5 Megatrends for procurement

The 5 trends are:

  • Gen AI
    • 72% report AI as top tech priority. Move to Agentic (self-learning) and multi-modality (many ways to use) will increase usage and change the way procurement delivers its services
  • Energy Transition
    • Sector undergoing seismic shift. Needs to be treated as critical spend category
  • Global Divisions
    • Geopolitics changing supply chains, driving them shorter and more regional
  • Regulatory Complexity
    • Increasing legislation top of mind, and also driving supply chains shorter
  • Generational Shifts
    • Mass retirements coming up will change the nature of the workforce, and as workforce gets younger, then tolerance of technology (like AI) increases.

Top technology trends

59% of procurement leaders believe that having up-to-date technology will have a significant impact on procurement performance

74% of respondents named AI and generative AI (GenAI), 68% named data and analytics, and 22% named other emerging technologies as one of their top three technology priorities for 2025.

Upgrading or optimising current technology is the overall top choice.

 

P2P Automation impacts on procurement as a function

Automation reduces the need for manual, transactional tasks, enabling procurement to focus on strategic activities such as category management, strategic sourcing and supplier relationship management. Increasing P2P automation can create opportunities for specialized roles focused on high-impact activities like supplier collaboration, optimized sourcing and data-driven decision making. Automation may transform procurement operating models by enabling centralization, standardization and enhanced collaboration across departments, while allowing for strategic decisions between maintaining an in-house model versus outsourcing — ultimately leading to a more strategic and efficient procurement operating model.

 

How Procurement leads

The 2024 Gartner Chief Procurement Officer Survey asked chief procurement officers  about their top value propositions in 2025 and 2030

Cost reduction is currently cited as the most important value proposition by 52%, signalling that procurement is clawing back price increases after years of inflationary pressure. The future value proposition of cost reduction is ranked lower.

Risk management and resilience are cited by 43% of CPOs as an important value proposition for procurement in 2025, as its operating environment has been inundated with risks that have varied in size, complexity and speed. This holds true for CPOs in the long term, which reiterates that procurement must be resilient in navigating emerging and unknown risks by proactively prioritizing the risk factors that need immediate attention

Sustainability is cited by 37% of CPOs as a top-three procurement value proposition for 2025. This indicates that an increasingly challenging regulatory landscape, and heightened customer expectations are prompting CPOs to embed ESG requirements into how procurement operates. This proposition remains a priority in 2030, indicating that procurement must reposition itself — from meeting imminent regulatory demands to advancing sustainable procurement goals that move the needle on organizational targets

All very similar to the Economist Impact study on procurement released earlier this year.

Economist Impact -  Supply Chains

This report sponsored by GEP covers supply chains and how they may evolve over the future. Its is based on a survey of 400 supply chain execs in US and Europe. The continuing core essentiality of supply chains to their business is continuing to be a primary concern for many with the following as stand out takeaways:

  • 55% of executives anticipate significant supply-chain disruptions, as they are caught between macroeconomic uncertainty, government protectionism and the impacts of climate change
  • 63% of executives flagged macroeconomic uncertainty and geopolitics as major supply chain disruptors
  • 38% of executives view procurement and supply-chain decisions as having a major influence on business strategy
  • 73% identified that improving supplier relationships is essential for supply chain strategy.
  • 57% of businesses plan to invest in AI and 44% in advanced automation to enhance their supply-chain and procurement operations

 

McKinsey Supply Chains – Still Vulnerable

Continuing the supply chain theme comes this article from McKinsey.  As the title suggests it covers the vulnerability of supply chains (something identified by the SAP Economist impact study as well) with 90% from their pulse survey stating they had encountered supply chain challenges this year.

Some of the longer term procurement priorities that Economist Impact identified are being worked on by companies now. 73% reported progress on dual sources of supply (Multi- sourcing) and 60% are moving to regionalise supply chains (Re/near shoring).

Inventory strategy remains split between 47% who want to keep their inventories art current levels, and 46% planning to reduce levels closer to pre pandemic levels.

Getting deeper transparency into the n tier struggles to make an impact according to the report which is interesting when reviewing the Gartner work earlier.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

KPMG CEO Outlook

The 10th edition of the KPMG CEO Outlook, conducted with 1,325 CEOs , provides an interesting insight into the mindset, strategies and planning tactics of CEOs.

 

 

Well over half (61 percent) identified ethical challenges as some of the most difficult issues to address when implementing AI within their businesses — an increase from 57 percent in 2023. Additionally, concerns over a lack of regulation (50 percent) and insufficient technical skills and capabilities (48 percent) further complicate the path forward.

Confidence in the global economy has remained stable over the last 3 years around the early 70% mark, but a lot lower than 10 years ago when it was over 90%.

This additional pressure felt by CEOs could be attributed to an evolving list of threats to business growth with this year’s survey showing that CEOs are the most concerned about the impact of supply chain disruption, and operational issues on their business’ growth in the next three years, coming in above cyber security and even last year’s number one threat – geopolitics and political uncertainty.

World CC Most Negotiated Terms

This annual study sponsored by Icertis, obtained responses from over 900 organisations and provides a good insight into what’s being negotiated, where issues may still be and as the report shows the disconnect between what’s important, and what’s being negotiated!

A key observation is that, in spite of the many shifts in the business environment over the last 20  years, in particular a growing focus on outcomes, the negotiation agenda has not kept pace.

There is a disconnect between what negotiators focus on, i.e. the Most Negotiated Terms versus where problems tend to emerge, i.e. the Most Disputed Terms. There is a similar disconnect between Most Negotiated Terms and Most Important Terms

Most negotiated top 3 are

  • Limitation of liability
  • Price changes and prices
  • Scope/Spec

Most disputed are

  • Prices and price changes
  • Delivery
  • Scope and spec

Most Important are

  • Scope/spec
  • Prices and price changes
  • Delivery

A significant portion of negotiation time is spent on terms that may never be invoked unless something goes wrong and delivery, which is both disputed and most important top 3 doesn’t make the top 3 of most negotiated.

As always reach out to discuss more, and always happy to hear your thoughts!

entry.title: Unlocking Value in Mergers, Acquisitions, and Divestitures through SAP: A Key Enabler for CPG
entry.content:
Authors: Ayten Erbektas, Katie Nistad, Dmitry Melnik, SAP

Unlocking Value in Mergers, Acquisitions, and Divestitures through SAP: A Key Enabler for the Consumer Packaged Goods Industry

Heated debate has been ongoing for almost three hours. Ceylan, Head of Marketing and Sales, reflected on the Board’s deliberations to sell one of the company’s business units amidst recently declining returns. After all, she was the one who recommended to buy that brand almost a decade ago. But times change as do consumer preferences – and these products no longer experienced high demand across all retail locations of the company’s partners. Yesterday, Ceylan proposed to the Board another acquisition, of a high-promising consumer brand which would allow the company not only to reinvigorate its portfolio and consumer appeal, but also to diversify into an adjacent market. Directors gazed intently at Ceylan: it was her time to answer the key question of the day, “How ready are the company’s systems and processes to execute such a divestiture and digest such an acquisition?”

Our blog examines this topic and summarizes the experiences of multiple SAP customers who constantly rebalance their portfolio with the help of SAP solutions.

*             *             *

In today’s rapidly evolving Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) industry, corporate combinations have emerged as a central growth strategy. Mergers, acquisitions, and divestitures (M&A/D) enable companies to expand their market reach, enter new segments, and drive revenue growth. However, managing these processes effectively is no easy feat. Companies face numerous challenges, from long due diligence processes and transition delays to under-realized synergies. This is where SAP S/4HANA, a powerful ERP system, can play a transformative role in helping organizations unlock the full potential of their M&A/D activities. Moreover, SAP solutions help monitor the optimal composition of the corporate portfolio: does brand A continue to perform? are we detecting early signs of the performance deterioration in brand B? why does brand C – while performing as usual – require even increasing investments which lower its ROI?  These and many similar questions are addressed by SAP solutions in real-time.

The Growing Role of M&A/D in the CPG Industry

In an industry characterized by fierce competition and shifting consumer preferences, M&A has become an essential tool for companies to remain competitive and grow their market share. Many CPG companies invest billions in acquiring new businesses to enter different markets, diversify their product offerings, or increase revenue. At the same time, divesting from non-core assets helps streamline operations and focus on strategic goals. Picture below displays M&A/D experience of just one of many SAP customers: it shows clearly that M&A/D is a big deal.

 

While the potential benefits of M&A are substantial, the process of integrating acquired businesses is complex and fraught with challenges. The integration of disparate systems, aligning corporate cultures, and realizing synergies in operations and costs are just a few of the hurdles companies must overcome. In many cases, companies struggle to meet these integration goals, leading to missed opportunities and suboptimal returns on investment. Transition Service Agreements (TSA) customarily executed between the buyer and the seller also present a significant cost item: the example above shows that just 10 acquisitions in 16 years cost the acquiror $470m in TSA and other PMI costs – in addition to over $8bn in purchase price.

To address these challenges, forward-thinking companies are turning to SAP S/4HANA, an ERP system designed to support the full M&A lifecycle—from due diligence to post-merger integration. SAP S/4HANA’s capabilities enable organizations to streamline processes, reduce redundant operations, and ensure data consistency, thus accelerating the integration process and unlocking value more efficiently. Many other SAP solutions provide solid foundation to execute M&A/D, including SAP Analytics Cloud, PaPM, SAP MDM and others. But to keep things simple, we will focus on SAP S/4HANA.

SAP S/4HANA: A Catalyst for Successful M&A Integration

Based on our experience in supporting customers’ strategic investments in SAP technology, we have seen how SAP S/4HANA can significantly enhance M&A outcomes. One of the key lessons from my previous role in post-merger integration is that the success of an M&A often hinges on how quickly and effectively a company can integrate its new acquisition into its existing operations.

 

The picture above is derived from the M&A experiences of many SAP customers and shows typical deal execution and consummation phases. So, how do customers utilize SAP solutions for their inorganic growth and portfolio rebalancing?  SAP S/4HANA provides a structured framework to manage M&A activities from pre-transaction to post-merger integration. Let’s break down how this system supports the three critical phases of integration:

  1. Addressing Immediate Priorities: One of the most urgent challenges after an acquisition is financial reporting. SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting simplifies this process by enabling companies to consolidate financial data from multiple systems, regardless of whether the acquired business uses SAP or another ERP. This ensures timely, accurate reporting and compliance, which is essential during the early stages of integration.
  2. Realizing Synergies: Once the immediate reporting requirements are met, the next step is to realize the synergies that motivated the acquisition. SAP S/4HANA helps organizations streamline operations by identifying and eliminating redundant processes, integrating procurement and supply chain functions, and extending shared services to the newly acquired entity. Automation is a key enabler in this phase, allowing companies to reduce manual tasks and improve efficiency.
  3. Driving Long-Term Growth: After the operational synergies are achieved, companies can focus on long-term growth by optimizing production, procurement, and other key business functions. SAP S/4HANA’s advanced analytics and production planning tools, such as MRP Live, allow organizations to make data-driven decisions that enhance efficiency, reduce costs, and improve product quality. This helps companies leverage their new acquisitions to drive growth and expand into new markets.

Flexibility in Cloud Deployment: Tailored to Your Needs

One of SAP S/4HANA’s standout features is its flexibility in deployment. Whether through public or private cloud models, it provides companies with the agility to tailor their ERP system to meet the specific needs of each acquisition. Smaller acquisitions can be onboarded quickly and efficiently through the S/4HANA public cloud, ensuring faster integration at a lower cost. For larger, more complex acquisitions, the private cloud offers more customization and control, allowing companies to address sophisticated processes while maintaining standardization where needed.

This two-tiered cloud architecture is ideal for companies that regularly engage in M&A activities, as it offers a scalable and adaptable solution to meet the varying demands of different acquisitions. SAP S/4HANA enables companies to onboard smaller acquisitions quickly while reserving the more robust private cloud for larger, more intricate integrations.

Success Stories: Driving M&A Value with SAP S/4HANA

Many leading companies have successfully used SAP S/4HANA to optimize their M&A processes and realize significant value from their acquisitions. For instance, global organizations in industries ranging from fashion to manufacturing have extended their shared services and automated key processes through SAP S/4HANA, driving operational efficiencies and enabling faster growth.

By integrating disparate systems, standardizing data, and automating core functions such as procurement and finance, these companies have been able to reduce costs, improve reporting accuracy, and enhance decision-making capabilities. The result? Greater agility, faster time-to-market, and improved financial performance post-acquisition.

 

The Future of M&A with SAP S/4HANA

As the CPG industry continues to evolve, consolidation will remain a key strategy for growth. Companies that can successfully navigate the complexities of M&A will be well-positioned to thrive in this dynamic environment. SAP S/4HANA offers the tools needed to unlock the full potential of M&A, enabling companies to integrate acquisitions more smoothly, realize synergies faster, and drive long-term value.

With its flexible cloud deployment options, advanced analytics, and automation capabilities, SAP S/4HANA provides the foundation for companies to stay agile, profitable, and ahead of the curve in their M&A endeavors.

Conclusion: Empowering M&A Success with SAP S/4HANA

Mergers, acquisitions, and divestitures are powerful tools for driving growth, but they also come with significant challenges. Companies need a robust and flexible ERP system to navigate these complexities and maximize the value of their M&A activities. SAP S/4HANA is designed to do just that—helping businesses streamline integration, unlock synergies, and position themselves for long-term growth.

In short, SAP S/4HANA is not just another ERP system—it’s a strategic enabler that empowers companies to realize the full value of their M&A strategies and stay competitive in the fast-changing CPG industry.

For those ready to explore how SAP S/4HANA can support their M&A journey, the opportunity is now. Unlock the value, stay agile, and drive growth with the right tools in place.

 

*             *             *

The Board voted unanimously.  Both the divestiture and the acquisition were approved and would happen very soon. Ceylan felt that this decision also marked the vote of confidence in her own team, which JAD relentlessly implemented modern IT solutions throughout the entire company. She sat in front of her team and business owners assigned to this acquisition.  Much work was ahead of them in the post-merger integration. But this would take the company to the next level in product and geographic scope. And there was so much else to accomplish throughout the PMI!,,,”

entry.title: Re: Custom Learning LxP Integration with SAP SuccessFactors LMS
entry.content:
This is a nice article, but in my experience integrating an LxP with an LMS (such as EdCast to an LMS), one critical element missing here is learning plan integration. Without this, SF LMS functions more as a database without leveraging its LMS capabilities. Learning plan integration can be particularly challenging for two reasons: (1) there are no APIs available, so custom reports must be created for this purpose, and (2) differentiating between training and retraining can be complex to implement in PRD SQL.

Overall, a good article.

entry.title: Unlocking Value in Mergers, Acquisitions, and Divestitures through SAP: A Key Enabler for CPG
entry.content:
Authors: Ayten Erbektas, Katie Nistad, Dmitry Melnik, SAP

Unlocking Value in Mergers, Acquisitions, and Divestitures through SAP: A Key Enabler for the Consumer Packaged Goods Industry

Heated debate has been ongoing for almost three hours. Ceylan, Head of Marketing and Sales, reflected on the Board’s deliberations to sell one of the company’s business units amidst recently declining returns. After all, she was the one who recommended to buy that brand almost a decade ago. But times change as do consumer preferences – and these products no longer experienced high demand across all retail locations of the company’s partners. Yesterday, Ceylan proposed to the Board another acquisition, of a high-promising consumer brand which would allow the company not only to reinvigorate its portfolio and consumer appeal, but also to diversify into an adjacent market. Directors gazed intently at Ceylan: it was her time to answer the key question of the day, “How ready are the company’s systems and processes to execute such a divestiture and digest such an acquisition?”

Our blog examines this topic and summarizes the experiences of multiple SAP customers who constantly rebalance their portfolio with the help of SAP solutions.

*             *             *

In today’s rapidly evolving Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) industry, corporate combinations have emerged as a central growth strategy. Mergers, acquisitions, and divestitures (M&A/D) enable companies to expand their market reach, enter new segments, and drive revenue growth. However, managing these processes effectively is no easy feat. Companies face numerous challenges, from long due diligence processes and transition delays to under-realized synergies. This is where SAP S/4HANA, a powerful ERP system, can play a transformative role in helping organizations unlock the full potential of their M&A/D activities. Moreover, SAP solutions help monitor the optimal composition of the corporate portfolio: does brand A continue to perform? are we detecting early signs of the performance deterioration in brand B? why does brand C – while performing as usual – require even increasing investments which lower its ROI?  These and many similar questions are addressed by SAP solutions in real-time.

The Growing Role of M&A/D in the CPG Industry

In an industry characterized by fierce competition and shifting consumer preferences, M&A has become an essential tool for companies to remain competitive and grow their market share. Many CPG companies invest billions in acquiring new businesses to enter different markets, diversify their product offerings, or increase revenue. At the same time, divesting from non-core assets helps streamline operations and focus on strategic goals. Picture below displays M&A/D experience of just one of many SAP customers: it shows clearly that M&A/D is a big deal.

 

While the potential benefits of M&A are substantial, the process of integrating acquired businesses is complex and fraught with challenges. The integration of disparate systems, aligning corporate cultures, and realizing synergies in operations and costs are just a few of the hurdles companies must overcome. In many cases, companies struggle to meet these integration goals, leading to missed opportunities and suboptimal returns on investment. Transition Service Agreements (TSA) customarily executed between the buyer and the seller also present a significant cost item: the example above shows that just 10 acquisitions in 16 years cost the acquiror $470m in TSA and other PMI costs – in addition to over $8bn in purchase price.

To address these challenges, forward-thinking companies are turning to SAP S/4HANA, an ERP system designed to support the full M&A lifecycle—from due diligence to post-merger integration. SAP S/4HANA’s capabilities enable organizations to streamline processes, reduce redundant operations, and ensure data consistency, thus accelerating the integration process and unlocking value more efficiently. Many other SAP solutions provide solid foundation to execute M&A/D, including SAP Analytics Cloud, PaPM, SAP MDM and others. But to keep things simple, we will focus on SAP S/4HANA.

SAP S/4HANA: A Catalyst for Successful M&A Integration

Based on our experience in supporting customers’ strategic investments in SAP technology, we have seen how SAP S/4HANA can significantly enhance M&A outcomes. One of the key lessons from my previous role in post-merger integration is that the success of an M&A often hinges on how quickly and effectively a company can integrate its new acquisition into its existing operations.

 

The picture above is derived from the M&A experiences of many SAP customers and shows typical deal execution and consummation phases. So, how do customers utilize SAP solutions for their inorganic growth and portfolio rebalancing?  SAP S/4HANA provides a structured framework to manage M&A activities from pre-transaction to post-merger integration. Let’s break down how this system supports the three critical phases of integration:

  1. Addressing Immediate Priorities: One of the most urgent challenges after an acquisition is financial reporting. SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting simplifies this process by enabling companies to consolidate financial data from multiple systems, regardless of whether the acquired business uses SAP or another ERP. This ensures timely, accurate reporting and compliance, which is essential during the early stages of integration.
  2. Realizing Synergies: Once the immediate reporting requirements are met, the next step is to realize the synergies that motivated the acquisition. SAP S/4HANA helps organizations streamline operations by identifying and eliminating redundant processes, integrating procurement and supply chain functions, and extending shared services to the newly acquired entity. Automation is a key enabler in this phase, allowing companies to reduce manual tasks and improve efficiency.
  3. Driving Long-Term Growth: After the operational synergies are achieved, companies can focus on long-term growth by optimizing production, procurement, and other key business functions. SAP S/4HANA’s advanced analytics and production planning tools, such as MRP Live, allow organizations to make data-driven decisions that enhance efficiency, reduce costs, and improve product quality. This helps companies leverage their new acquisitions to drive growth and expand into new markets.

Flexibility in Cloud Deployment: Tailored to Your Needs

One of SAP S/4HANA’s standout features is its flexibility in deployment. Whether through public or private cloud models, it provides companies with the agility to tailor their ERP system to meet the specific needs of each acquisition. Smaller acquisitions can be onboarded quickly and efficiently through the S/4HANA public cloud, ensuring faster integration at a lower cost. For larger, more complex acquisitions, the private cloud offers more customization and control, allowing companies to address sophisticated processes while maintaining standardization where needed.

This two-tiered cloud architecture is ideal for companies that regularly engage in M&A activities, as it offers a scalable and adaptable solution to meet the varying demands of different acquisitions. SAP S/4HANA enables companies to onboard smaller acquisitions quickly while reserving the more robust private cloud for larger, more intricate integrations.

Success Stories: Driving M&A Value with SAP S/4HANA

Many leading companies have successfully used SAP S/4HANA to optimize their M&A processes and realize significant value from their acquisitions. For instance, global organizations in industries ranging from fashion to manufacturing have extended their shared services and automated key processes through SAP S/4HANA, driving operational efficiencies and enabling faster growth.

By integrating disparate systems, standardizing data, and automating core functions such as procurement and finance, these companies have been able to reduce costs, improve reporting accuracy, and enhance decision-making capabilities. The result? Greater agility, faster time-to-market, and improved financial performance post-acquisition.

 

The Future of M&A with SAP S/4HANA

As the CPG industry continues to evolve, consolidation will remain a key strategy for growth. Companies that can successfully navigate the complexities of M&A will be well-positioned to thrive in this dynamic environment. SAP S/4HANA offers the tools needed to unlock the full potential of M&A, enabling companies to integrate acquisitions more smoothly, realize synergies faster, and drive long-term value.

With its flexible cloud deployment options, advanced analytics, and automation capabilities, SAP S/4HANA provides the foundation for companies to stay agile, profitable, and ahead of the curve in their M&A endeavors.

Conclusion: Empowering M&A Success with SAP S/4HANA

Mergers, acquisitions, and divestitures are powerful tools for driving growth, but they also come with significant challenges. Companies need a robust and flexible ERP system to navigate these complexities and maximize the value of their M&A activities. SAP S/4HANA is designed to do just that—helping businesses streamline integration, unlock synergies, and position themselves for long-term growth.

In short, SAP S/4HANA is not just another ERP system—it’s a strategic enabler that empowers companies to realize the full value of their M&A strategies and stay competitive in the fast-changing CPG industry.

For those ready to explore how SAP S/4HANA can support their M&A journey, the opportunity is now. Unlock the value, stay agile, and drive growth with the right tools in place.

 

*             *             *

The Board voted unanimously.  Both the divestiture and the acquisition were approved and would happen very soon. Ceylan felt that this decision also marked the vote of confidence in her own team, which relentlessly implemented modern IT solutions throughout the entire company. She sat in front of her team and business owners assigned to this acquisition.  Much work was ahead of them in the post-merger integration. But this would take the company to the next level in product and geographic scope. And there was so much else to accomplish throughout the PMI!,,,”

entry.title: 2H 2024: Enhancing the Candidate Experience with Embedded AI in SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting
entry.content:
With the 2H 2024 release, we have delivered new AI-driven capabilities within SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting to improve the candidate experience and accelerate skills-based hiring.

Through customer influence on the Customer Community and innovative product enhancements, we are bringing your ideas to life and driving improvements across the recruiting experience.

To learn more watch the on-demand Q&A webinar: 2H 2024 Product Release Webinars.

All of the 2H 2024 release items can be found on the What’s New Viewer. These new features will be live in Production on November 17th, 2024.

Now, explore a preview of the top highlights in SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting to get excited about in 2H 2024: 

AI-Assisted Job Recommendations

With this release, candidates can now use AI to discover jobs that match the skills they have, enabling them to find and apply for the right opportunities for them. When candidates upload their resumes to the career site, AI extracts their skills, allowing candidates to discover open roles that match their skills.

Allowing candidates to participate in skills-based hiring helps drive adoption, improve talent pools, and encourage valuable talent to find the right opportunities for them. Candidates can view, validate, and modify AI-extracted skills, enabling them to have control over the skills-based job recommendations, driving them to the most relevant opportunities for them.

Candidates can view AI-driven job recommendations based on the skills extracted from their resume, aligned to skills needed for various roles. This encourages candidates to consider more opportunities that align to their skills.

AI-Assisted Job Skills on Career Site

When candidates find a role they are interested in, they can view the job details page, which now shows AI-assisted job skills and provides insights into matching and missing skills. The AI-assisted skills cloud on job details pages gives candidates a clear view into the skills needed for a role, enabling them to apply for roles that best fit their skill set.

Simplified Candidate Authentication

As part of an ongoing initiative to deliver a simplified candidate experience, external candidate authentication enables applicants to create a profile and apply for a role with a phone number or social account login, instead of an email address. Candidates can complete the application process more easily and benefit from an improved user experience as familiar as their favorite sites. Candidates can begin applying for a role by simply registering with a social account or text login. These enhancements help deliver a quick, mobile-first experience to secure interested candidates, reducing candidate drop-off, accelerating time to apply, and improving the overall candidate experience for deskless applicants.

Delivering Enhancements to the Recruiter Experience   

With each release, we continue to deliver enhancements to the redesigned applicant management tool, providing recruiters with sophisticated tools and AI-driven capabilities to quickly screen, manage, and hire applicants. With this release, we have enhanced the AI-assisted skills matching feature to let you view the total number of skills in an applicant’s resume that match the skills specified as primary or secondary on the job description. This enhancement helps recruiters and hiring managers utilize the skills compatibility insights to make informed decisions during applicant screening and hiring.

Continuous upcoming enhancements to the applicant management tool will be delivered with each release, ensuring key functionality is available to customers ahead of the depreciation timeline of the legacy applicant workbench tool planned for 2026.

Additionally, for recruiting postings, we continue to extend the list of job board integrations available in the job board marketplace to fulfill continuous recruitment needs regionally.

With every release, we are excited to deliver these new innovations and continue our journey of providing even greater value to our SAP SuccessFactors customers. For full details, please check out the 2H 2024 Release Highlights Resources: 

  • 2H 2024 Release Highlights Brochure 
  • 2H 2024 Release Highlights Video 
  • Product Release & Road Map Information 

Learn more about SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting. 

SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Activate Retention Process with PO in sap s4 h...
entry.content:
In the purchase order, you define a retention as a percentage of the purchase order value at header or item level. The system calculates the retention for an invoice item based on this percentage, and proposes the retention amount that it calculates in this way when you enter the invoice. You can overwrite all default values for the retention amount in the invoice.

Please refer to this online documentation for detailed information:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/676fb6531de6b64ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

Also please refer to this blog about the retention process:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/retention-process-configuration-its-process-flow/ba-p/13524615

entry.title: Re: Profit center in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @ErikVH 

There is no ability to deactivate use of Profit Centers in S/4HANA Cloud Public. 

However you can minimize the maintenance by only defining a single PC and assigning to all records.

SAP recommends use of PCs to provide additional breakdown of Balance Sheet, for margin reporting and to provide a view across a range of cost object types such as Cost Centers, Projects and Orders.

regards

Mike

entry.title: Second Half 2024 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits?
entry.content:
The Second Half 2024 Release is here! I wanted to take just a couple of minutes to share with you some exciting news about SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits.

As you may already know, the First Half 2024 Release introduced a new guided benefits enrollment experience to help simplify and streamline the experience for employees. Met with excitement by existing customers eager to adopt the new experience, and described as a game-changer by those evaluating SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits, the refreshed enrollment experience makes benefits enrollment more seamless, simple and intuitive for employees.

However, we didn’t stop there! Building upon the user experience enhancements in our First Half 2024 Release, we continue to enhance the global benefits experience to make it easier for employees to enroll in and manage their benefits.

Refreshed Benefits Overview Page                                                                                                                 With the Second Half 2024 Release, we are excited to introduce a refreshed benefits overview page for insurance, savings, and pension benefit types. This new experience, available on desktop and mobile devices, provides employes with a single place to view their benefits details and take action such as declaring a life event, managing their beneficiaries, or enrolling in benefits. And, with convenient access from the ‘Manage My Benefits’ quick action card on the home page, the benefits overview enables employees to easily find a summary of their enrolled benefits, view their confirmation statement, and see available benefits.

Refreshed Benefits Overview Page (Desktop)

The updated Benefits Overview page consolidates benefits activities that were previously distributed across the employee profile and legacy benefits overview. As part of the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile application, the new benefits overview provides employees access to all the features of the desktop experience from the convenience of their mobile devices.

Refreshed Benefits Overview Page (Mobile)

Benefits Engagement Cards                                                                                                                                  In addition, we’ve further improved the benefits experience with new engagement cards to notify employees of time sensitive benefits-related tasks such as benefits enrollment windows for new hires, rehires and open enrollment. With a countdown of days remaining in the enrollment windows, these new engagement cards prompt employees to take action with simplified navigation to the enrollment process directly from the home page. 

Benefits Engagement Cards on the SAP SuccessFactors Homepage

But that’s not all… access the What’s new viewer to learn about all the innovations available in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits with the Second Half 2024 Release.

For More Information                                                                                                                                          To learn about all the enhancements and innovations available across the SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite in the SAP SuccessFactors Second Half 2024 Release, check out the resources below:

  • Read the 2H 2024 Release Highlights Brochure
  • Watch the 2H 2024 Release Highlights Video, featuring Bianka Woelke, GVP Head of Application Product Management

entry.title: Re: SAC Data Action Advanced Formula - Copy Actual of Las...
entry.content:
Hi @SyedShoaib 

First, set the external parameter to get the Last updated month as shown in the screenshot below,

Second, set the Planning period for the Plan Version in the Version Property "Start" & "End",

- The Planning period value should be in Date format (YYYYMM: 202412) for Start & End.

- Start property & value is not mandatory.

 

The Advanced Formulas Script to get your expected result,

 

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = NEXT(1, "MONTH", %Last_Updated_Month%) TO [d/Version].[p/End]

DATA() = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version]="public.Actual", [d/Date]=%Last_Updated_Month%)

 

 

I hope this is helpful to you, and if I have misunderstood anything, please feel free to reach out.

Moonjun

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager Licensing: What You Need to Know about Mobile Services Entitlements
entry.content:
SAP Service and Asset Manager is a mobile application designed for maintenance and field service execution. Deployed on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Mobile Services, it provides customers with a robust, standard application that includes pre-defined business objects, enterprise asset management content, service management content, and seamless integration capabilities—offering a valuable alternative to building a custom mobile solution from scratch.

The app is deployed on BTP Mobile Services to leverage multiple powerful features, such as the SDK/MDK extension framework, Cloud Connector integration for backend connectivity, and offline functionality. Since BTP Mobile Services is fundamental to the app's architecture, it has been bundled into the licensing model of SAP Service and Asset Manager. Over the years, this licensing model has evolved alongside the product itself. Here’s a look at the progression of material codes descriptions for SAP Service and Asset Manager since its initial release:

  1. SAP Asset Manager for ERP
  2. SAP Asset Manager
  3. SAP Service and Asset Manager
  4. SAP Mobile Execution and Dynamic Forms

If you’re an existing customer, your specific material code depends on the start date of your contract, but each of these codes historically included an equivalent number of BTP Mobile Services users as those licensed for SAP Service and Asset Manager. 

The evolution of SAP Service and Asset Manager’s licensing has coincided with changes in BTP Mobile Services plans. Various service plans have been introduced or deprecated, leading us to the focus of this post:

What Does “Deprecated” Mean for the BTP Mobile Services Standard Plan?

You may have noticed the “standard” plan in your BTP environment labeled as “deprecated.” If you hold an active contract with one of the former material codes, you can continue using the standard plan until your contract renewal date. The deprecation notice won’t impact your technical usage.

If you don’t want to use the deprecated Mobile Services standard plan, consider to replace your existing material code with the latest one, SAP Mobile Execution and Dynamic Forms. This includes usage rights to basic-app-plus plan for internal SAP Applications.

What to Expect When Renewing Your Contract

Upon renewal, your sales representative will update your material code to the latest version, named SAP Mobile Execution and Dynamic Forms. The BTP Mobile Services team has also introduced a new service plan called “basic-app-plus,” specifically tailored for SAP internal applications like SAP Service and Asset Manager. 

Exception for Neo Customers

For customers still operating on a Neo environment, which is scheduled for sunset in 2028, there is an exception. Neo access rights are granted only for the older SKUs. If you’re planning a migration to Cloud Foundry in the coming years, your sales team can work with you to secure approval for continued use of the previous material codes. More details about the Neo phase-out are available in this SAP blog post.

Key Steps for Customers with CPEA+Subscription-Based or PAYG+Subscription-Based Global Accounts

If you have a CPEA agreement with a subscription-based BTP global account, it’s essential to select either the “standard” or “basic-app-plus” plan when using SAP Service and Asset Manager. Otherwise, usage may trigger additional billing for BTP Mobile Services. For instance, if you licensed the app under material code named SAP Service and Asset Manager but opted for the resource plan instead of the standard plan, Mobile Services may bill you as if you were developing a custom app.

Setting Up the Basic-App-Plus Plan for New and Existing Users

For new customers using the latest material code, SAP Mobile Execution and Dynamic Forms, or existing customers switching to this code, follow these steps to ensure you’re leveraging the right entitlements:

  1. Log into your BTP subaccount where your Mobile Services entitlements are provisioned.
  2. (Optional) Verify the source of your entitlements.

 

  1. Go to Entitlements > Edit > Add Service Plans, search for Mobile Services, and select the basic-app-plus service plan for your global account.
  2. Access Instances and Subscriptions in Mobile Services, select an app with license type set to resources or standard, and change it to basic-app-plus (available only if the entitlements are configured correctly).

Step by step video here: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/qQuwTM2?rc=10&doi=SAP1142392 

For new app creation, there’s a streamlined experience in Mobile Services. Create a new app and, when choosing a custom app, specify SAP Service and Asset Manager as an internal SAP application—an option available only with the basic-app-plus plan.

Alternatively, if you were using the app create program from the backend to automatically create your new application in BTP, the prompt is now defaulting the basic-app-plus plan as well. Details here: 3504991 - SAP Service and Asset Manager 2410 Mobile Services App Create Updates

Additional Licensing Updates

With the latest material code (SAP Mobile Execution and Dynamic Forms), customers now receive additional BTP entitlements:

  • 3 users/month for SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)
  • A fixed entitlement of SAP Dynamic Forms (documents/FUE User)

These additions expand on the services included with previous SKUs (3GB of Cloud Foundry runtime and BTP Mobile Services), ensuring that customers have all necessary tools to operate SAP Service and Asset Manager effectively without requiring extra BTP service licenses.

For additional questions feel free to reach out or talk to your sales representative.

SAP Service and Asset Manager SAP Mobile Services SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: Re: CDS Annotations: How to not display a field on the List Report Column but keep then on the OData
entry.content: To hide a field in the UI but retain it in metadata for business calculations in OData v2 with Fiori Elements, try using @Consumption.hidden: true. This annotation hides the field in the UI but keeps it available in the metadata, allowing it to be used in calculations without displaying it in the List Report. To learn more about effective Fiori Elements and OData practices, visit 5starsstocks .com.

entry.title: SAP Time Management T510S settings help
entry.content:
Hi experts, I have the following scenario that I would like to solve in T510S WITHOUT using DWS or Day Types.

I would like to solve it (if possible) using T510S Conditions Time.

The scenario is,

Any shift finishing between 8:00 and 18:00 gets no wage type. Example: shift starts 23:00, and finishes 8:30, no shift allowance.

Any shift finishing between 18:00 and 23:59 gets all worked hours at wage type 1ABC (20% shift allowance). Example: shift starts 13:00, finishes 21:30. Wage type 1ABC 8.5hrs

Any shift finishing between 24:00 and 07:59 gets all worked hours at wage type 1ABB (30% shift allowance). Example: shift starts 21:00, finishes 5:30. Wage type 1ABB 8.5hrs

I know I can solve this using DWS Class but I would like to see if this can be solved using T510S Conditions Time as time entries come from clocks.

Thanks......

entry.title: Re: How can I create Credit Memo in Real Estate Management?
entry.content:
Hi Onireke,

There are 2 ways to create a credit in RE-FX:

  1. If you have already made a posting e.g. 1000 p/m and you want to reduce that you can update the amount to e.g. 800 and the system will automatically create a 200 credit. You will get a warning message when you update the condition that is already posted because the system will create a retrospective posting. Also make sure what time period the credit is for because you might have to create a time slice which the system will automatically prompt for in the warning message that you will receive. Whenever you make your change ALWAYS simulate the cashflow first before saving so you can see the impact of your change. If it is not correct just exit without saving and redo your change.
  2. Normally customer will have a condition type for "rent" and a separate for "rent credit" or "abatement" or "discount" depending on what it is called locally but they all do the same. The configuration for the account symbols is configured in reverse than what is done for the rent condition type so that a manual reversal can be entered, without touching the original rent posting.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: How to Pass Dynamic Selection Field in SAP ABAP Submi...
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to execute RFBILA00 with specific profit center (PRCTR) values using WITH FREE SELECTIONS. However, when I run the code, the report executes but includes all profit centers, not just the one(s) I specified.

Here is my code:

DATA : trange TYPE rsds_trange,
      trange_line LIKE LINE OF trange,
      trange_frange_t_line LIKE LINE OF trange_line-frange_t,
      trange_frange_t_selopt_t_line LIKE LINE OF trange_frange_t_line-selopt_t,
      texpr TYPE rsds_texpr.

LOOP AT s_values INTO DATA(lv_prctr_value).
trange_line-tablename = 'FAGLFREESEL'.
trange_frange_t_line-fieldname = 'PRCTR'.
trange_frange_t_selopt_t_line-sign = 'I'.
trange_frange_t_selopt_t_line-option = 'EQ'.
trange_frange_t_selopt_t_line-low = lv_prctr_value.
IF lv_prctr_value IS NOT INITIAL.
APPEND trange_frange_t_selopt_t_line TO trange_frange_t_line-selopt_t.
ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

APPEND trange_frange_t_line TO trange_line-frange_t.
APPEND trange_line TO trange.

CALL FUNCTION 'FREE_SELECTIONS_RANGE_2_EX'
EXPORTING
field_ranges = trange
IMPORTING
EXPRESSIONS = texpr.

SUBMIT rfbila00
WITH sd_ktopl-low = lv_ktopl
WITH sd_bukrs-low = lv_bukrs
WITH bilavers = '1000'
WITH bilaspra = lv_spras
WITH bilbjahr = lv_report_year
WITH B-MONATE = s_bilabmon
WITH bilvjahr = lv_compare_year
WITH V-MONATE = s_bilaVmon
WITH FREE SELECTIONS texpr
EXPORTING LIST TO MEMORY
AND RETURN.

Issue:

When I debug, I can see that teXpr is populated and passed to the SUBMIT statement, but my specified profit center values are not filtering the report. Instead, the report outputs data for all profit centers.

What I’ve Tried:

  1. I confirmed that the values in trange_frange_t_line are correct before calling FREE_SELECTIONS_RANGE_2_EX.
  2. I checked that the table name (FAGLFREESEL) and field name (PRCTR) align with the data structure in the report.

Any insights on why the FREE SELECTIONS aren’t applying the profit center filter correctly? I would greatly appreciate any suggestions on how to correctly format teXpr or troubleshoot further.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Profit center in Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi, Profit centers are organizational units that reflect a management-oriented view of the organization for the purposes of internal control. They allow you to analyze areas of responsibility and delegate responsibility to decentralized units, treating such units as "companies within the company." Profit center is the foundation for divisional accounting. I wondered why would you want to turn it off. If you could share what you expected and not expecting, we might could find other ways to achieve that. 'https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f0d042517a434458b171ff5867b18445/6baea0aebb1d4d54a1156f2698382c94.html best regards, liling
